text: BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic offers Sports Physiotherapy to athletes of all levels. Our treatment includes injury rehabilitation, performance enhancement, and preventative care. Price: 25,000 Naira per session.

Our Pediatric Physiotherapy service at BodyBalance focuses on developmental delays, cerebral palsy, and musculoskeletal issues in children. We provide a compassionate and supportive environment. Price: 20,000 Naira per session.

Ergonomics assessments at BodyBalance Clinic help corporate professionals prevent back and neck pain. We offer workplace setup advice and postural correction exercises. Corporate rates start at 50,000 Naira for 5 staff members.

Post-Operative Rehabilitation at BodyBalance ensures a safe recovery after surgeries such as ACL reconstruction or joint replacements. Our experts guide you through every step of healing. Price: 30,000 Naira per session.

Pain Management at BodyBalance Clinic uses advanced techniques like dry needling, manual therapy, and therapeutic exercises to manage chronic conditions like sciatica and fibromyalgia. Price: 25,000 Naira per session.
Booking an appointment at BodyBalance is easy. You can message us on WhatsApp at +234 800 BODY BAL or visit our clinic during working hours. Same-day appointments are often available.
BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic is located at 45 Wellness Avenue, Lagos, Nigeria. We are open Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM, and Saturdays from 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM.
Sciatica treatment at BodyBalance includes nerve gliding exercises, core strengthening, and posture education to relieve leg pain and numbness. Consultation fee: 10,000 Naira.
Frozen Shoulder (Adhesive Capsulitis) is managed at BodyBalance through progressive stretching, joint mobilization, and heat therapy to restore range of motion. Recovery usually takes 4-8 weeks.

Lower back pain is the most common condition treated at BodyBalance. We focus on identifying the root cause, whether it is a disc issue, muscle strain, or poor posture, and provide targeted exercises.
Knee injuries, including ligament tears and meniscus issues, are treated at BodyBalance with a focus on stability, strength, and gradual return to activity. We provide knee bracing advice.
Posture correction at BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic involves identifying muscle imbalances and prescribing specific exercises to correct 'tech neck' and slumping. We use digital postural analysis.
BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic provides home visits for patients who cannot travel to the clinic. Home service fee starts at 45,000 Naira depending on location within Lagos.
Sports massage at BodyBalance helps in muscle recovery, reducing tension, and improving flexibility. It is recommended for athletes after intense training or competition. Price: 15,000 Naira.
Manual therapy at BodyBalance involves hands-on techniques like joint mobilization and soft tissue manipulation to reduce pain and improve movement. It is included in standard sessions.
Dry needling at BodyBalance Clinic is an effective treatment for trigger points and muscle tightness. It is often combined with other physiotherapy techniques for better results.
Acupuncture for pain relief is available at BodyBalance Clinic, particularly for chronic back and neck pain. It is performed by certified physiotherapists.
Vestibular rehabilitation at BodyBalance helps patients suffering from dizziness, vertigo, and balance disorders. It involves specific eye and head movements.
Pelvic floor physiotherapy at BodyBalance Clinic addresses issues such as incontinence and pelvic pain in both men and women. We ensure absolute privacy and comfort.
Stroke rehabilitation at BodyBalance focuses on restoring movement, strength, and independence through intensive neuro-physiotherapy. Long-term packages are available.
Hydrotherapy advice is provided at BodyBalance for patients who benefit from water-based exercises, particularly for arthritis and severe joint pain.
BodyBalance Clinic offers custom orthotics and insoles for patients with flat feet, plantar fasciitis, or gait abnormalities. Prices start at 40,000 Naira.
TENS (Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation) is used at BodyBalance for non-invasive pain relief during recovery from injuries or chronic conditions.
Ultrasound therapy at BodyBalance Clinic promotes tissue healing and reduces inflammation in soft tissue injuries such as tendinitis or bursitis.
Cervical spondylosis treatment at BodyBalance involves neck mobilization, strengthening of deep neck flexors, and ergonomic advice to manage neck pain and stiffness.
Carpal tunnel syndrome is managed at BodyBalance through wrist splinting advice, nerve gliding, and workspace ergonomics to reduce pressure on the median nerve.
Plantar fasciitis relief at BodyBalance Clinic includes calf stretching, foot strengthening, and ultrasound therapy to reduce heel pain.
Scoliosis management at BodyBalance focus on specific exercises to improve spinal alignment and prevent progression in children and adults.
Osteoarthritis management at BodyBalance Clinic involves low-impact exercises, weight management advice, and joint protection techniques to improve quality of life.
BodyBalance provides pre-participation sports physicals to ensure athletes are fit to compete and to identify potential injury risks.
Workshops on workplace wellness and back care are organized by BodyBalance for corporate organizations across Nigeria.
BodyBalance Clinic uses evidence-based protocols for all treatments, ensuring that our patients receive the most effective care possible.
The consultation fee for new patients at BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic is 10,000 Naira, which includes a full assessment and treatment plan.
BodyBalance offers discounted packages for long-term rehabilitation cases, such as 10 sessions for the price of 9.
Patients can request their medical records and progress reports from BodyBalance Clinic for insurance or referral purposes.
BodyBalance Clinic accepts most major health insurance plans in Nigeria. Please check with our front desk for current affiliations.

If you have a sports injury, BodyBalance recommends the P.E.A.C.E & L.O.V.E protocol (Protection, Elevation, Avoid Anti-inflammatories, Compression, Education & Load, Optimism, Vascularisation, Exercise).
Chronic headache management at BodyBalance often involves treating the cervical spine (neck) and addressing postural issues that contribute to tension headaches.
BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic is committed to patient education, providing every patient with a home exercise program (HEP) to speed up recovery.
For knee pain, BodyBalance often recommends quadriceps strengthening and hip stability exercises to reduce the load on the knee joint.
To prevent back pain while working, BodyBalance suggests taking a 'micro-break' every 30 minutes to stretch and change position.

Physiotherapy is a healthcare profession that helps people restore, maintain, and maximize their physical function and movement. It uses physical methods like exercise, manual therapy, and education rather than drugs or surgery.

BodyBalance Physiotherapy treats a wide range of conditions including sports injuries, workplace injuries, post-surgical rehabilitation, neurological conditions, pediatric developmental issues, and chronic pain management.

Your first physiotherapy session at BodyBalance includes a comprehensive assessment where the therapist will ask about your medical history, current symptoms, and goals. They will then perform a physical examination and create a personalized treatment plan. Wear comfortable clothing that allows easy movement.

BodyBalance accepts multiple payment methods including cash, bank transfer, and major debit or credit cards. We also work with several HMOs and health insurance providers. Contact us to verify if your insurance is accepted.

If you need to cancel or reschedule your appointment at BodyBalance, please notify us at least 24 hours in advance. This allows us to offer your slot to another patient in need. Late cancellations may incur a fee.

Strengthening exercises help build muscle mass and support joints. They are a core component of physiotherapy treatment at BodyBalance and may include resistance bands, weights, or bodyweight exercises depending on your condition and fitness level.

Flexibility exercises improve range of motion and reduce muscle tension. At BodyBalance, stretching programs are tailored to your specific needs and may include static stretches, dynamic stretches, or proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation (PNF) techniques.

Balance and coordination exercises are important for preventing falls, especially in older adults. BodyBalance physiotherapists use exercises like single-leg stands, heel-to-toe walking, and stability ball work to improve balance.

Cardiovascular exercises in physiotherapy help improve heart health and endurance. At BodyBalance, low-impact options like walking, cycling, or swimming may be recommended as part of your rehabilitation program.

Postural exercises focus on strengthening core muscles and correcting alignment issues. Poor posture can contribute to neck pain, back pain, and headaches. BodyBalance offers postural assessments and corrective exercise programs.

Neurological physiotherapy at BodyBalance helps patients with conditions like stroke, Parkinson's disease, multiple sclerosis, and spinal cord injuries. Treatment focuses on retraining movement patterns and improving functional independence.

Geriatric physiotherapy addresses the unique needs of older adults, including fall prevention, arthritis management, osteoporosis care, and maintaining mobility for daily activities. BodyBalance offers specialized programs for seniors.

Women's health physiotherapy at BodyBalance covers pregnancy-related pain, postpartum recovery, pelvic floor dysfunction, and menopause-related musculoskeletal issues. Treatment is provided by specially trained female therapists.
Men's health physiotherapy at BodyBalance addresses prostate surgery recovery, pelvic pain, and sports injuries common in male athletes. Treatment plans are tailored to male-specific health concerns.

Prehabilitation or 'prehab' involves physiotherapy before surgery to strengthen muscles and improve fitness. Research shows prehab can lead to faster recovery times. BodyBalance offers pre-surgical conditioning programs.

Workplace physiotherapy at BodyBalance includes ergonomic assessments, workstation setup advice, and treatment for repetitive strain injuries. We help companies reduce sick leave and improve employee wellbeing.
Running injuries commonly treated at BodyBalance include runner's knee, shin splints, Achilles tendinitis, and plantar fasciitis. We offer gait analysis and running-specific rehabilitation programs.

Tennis and golfer's elbow are overuse injuries affecting the tendons in the forearm. BodyBalance treats these conditions with manual therapy, eccentric exercises, and advice on technique modification.
Whiplash injuries from car accidents often cause neck pain and stiffness. BodyBalance provides gentle mobilization, soft tissue work, and graduated exercise programs for whiplash recovery.
TMJ (temporomandibular joint) disorders can cause jaw pain, headaches, and difficulty eating. BodyBalance offers specialized physiotherapy including manual therapy and exercises for jaw dysfunction.
Fibromyalgia is a chronic condition characterized by widespread pain and fatigue. BodyBalance provides gentle exercise programs, pain management techniques, and education to help manage symptoms.
Chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) can benefit from carefully graded exercise therapy. BodyBalance physiotherapists specialize in pacing strategies and energy conservation techniques for CFS patients.
Lymphoedema is swelling caused by lymphatic system damage. BodyBalance offers manual lymphatic drainage, compression therapy, and education on self-management techniques.

Cardiac rehabilitation physiotherapy helps patients recover from heart attacks, heart surgery, or heart failure. BodyBalance provides supervised exercise programs to safely improve cardiovascular fitness.
Respiratory physiotherapy assists patients with breathing difficulties due to COPD, asthma, or post-COVID recovery. Techniques include breathing exercises, airway clearance, and chest physiotherapy.
Amputee rehabilitation at BodyBalance includes pre-prosthetic conditioning, prosthetic training, and gait re-education. We work closely with prosthetists for optimal outcomes.
Gait analysis at BodyBalance involves assessing how you walk or run to identify biomechanical issues. This can help diagnose problems and prevent future injuries.

Massage therapy at BodyBalance includes sports massage, deep tissue massage, and relaxation massage. Massage can complement physiotherapy treatment by reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation.

Heat therapy using hot packs or ultrasound can help relax tight muscles and improve blood flow. Cold therapy using ice packs can reduce inflammation and numb pain. BodyBalance uses both appropriately.

Electrotherapy modalities at BodyBalance include TENS for pain relief, ultrasound for tissue healing, and electrical muscle stimulation for strengthening. These are used as adjuncts to hands-on treatment.
Taping and strapping techniques at BodyBalance provide support to injured joints and muscles, facilitate proper movement patterns, and reduce pain during activity. Kinesio taping is also available.

Aquatic therapy or hydrotherapy uses water's buoyancy and resistance for rehabilitation. Exercises are easier on joints while still providing resistance for strengthening. BodyBalance can advise on pool programs.
Yoga and Pilates can complement physiotherapy by improving flexibility, core strength, and body awareness. BodyBalance may recommend specific poses or exercises from these disciplines.
Proper sleep posture is important for spinal health. BodyBalance advises on pillow height, mattress firmness, and sleeping positions to reduce neck and back pain.
Lifting technique is crucial for preventing back injuries. BodyBalance teaches proper lifting mechanics including bending at the knees, keeping loads close to the body, and avoiding twisting.
Driving posture can contribute to back and neck pain. BodyBalance provides advice on seat position, mirror adjustment, and taking regular breaks during long drives.
Pregnancy causes postural changes and can lead to back pain, pelvic girdle pain, and wrist pain. BodyBalance offers safe, effective treatment for expectant mothers throughout all trimesters.
Postpartum recovery at BodyBalance addresses diastasis recti, pelvic floor weakness, and gradual return to exercise. We help new mothers recover safely after childbirth.
Osteoporosis is a condition of weakened bones. BodyBalance provides weight-bearing and resistance exercises to help maintain bone density, along with fall prevention strategies.
Rheumatoid arthritis is an autoimmune condition affecting joints. BodyBalance physiotherapy helps manage pain, maintain joint mobility, and preserve function during flares and remissions.
Ankylosing spondylitis is an inflammatory condition affecting the spine. BodyBalance provides exercises to maintain spinal mobility and posture, along with breathing exercises.
Bursitis is inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs that cushion joints. Common sites include shoulder, hip, and knee. BodyBalance treats bursitis with rest, modalities, and gradual return to activity.
Tendinitis is inflammation of tendons often caused by overuse. BodyBalance uses eccentric loading exercises, manual therapy, and activity modification to promote tendon healing.
Muscle strains occur when muscle fibers are overstretched or torn. BodyBalance provides RICE protocol initially, then progressive loading to safely return to activity.
Ligament sprains involve stretching or tearing of ligaments. Severity ranges from mild (Grade 1) to complete tear (Grade 3). BodyBalance provides appropriate treatment at each stage.
Fracture rehabilitation at BodyBalance begins after medical clearance. We address stiffness, weakness, and functional limitations to help you return to normal activities safely.
Dislocation rehabilitation focuses on restoring range of motion, strength, and proprioception. BodyBalance provides graduated programs to prevent recurrence and optimize outcomes.
Concussion management has evolved to include physiotherapy for persistent symptoms. BodyBalance offers vestibular rehabilitation and graded exercise programs for post-concussion syndrome.
Dizziness and vertigo significantly impact quality of life. BodyBalance vestibular physiotherapy can help with BPPV, vestibular neuritis, and chronic dizziness conditions.
Incontinence affects many people but is treatable. BodyBalance pelvic floor physiotherapy helps with stress, urge, and mixed incontinence through targeted exercises.
Chronic prostatitis and pelvic pain in men can be helped with physiotherapy. BodyBalance offers pelvic floor muscle training and pain management techniques.
Endometriosis can cause significant pelvic pain. BodyBalance provides pain management strategies, gentle exercises, and techniques to help women manage this condition.
Breast cancer rehabilitation addresses post-surgical issues including shoulder stiffness, cording (axillary web syndrome), and lymphedema prevention. BodyBalance offers specialized care.
Prostate cancer rehabilitation at BodyBalance addresses incontinence and erectile dysfunction following surgery through pelvic floor exercises and education.
Cancer rehabilitation helps patients manage fatigue, maintain strength during treatment, and recover function afterward. BodyBalance provides safe, individualized programs.
Palliative care physiotherapy focuses on maintaining comfort, mobility, and quality of life for patients with serious illnesses. BodyBalance provides sensitive, patient-centered care.
Telehealth physiotherapy allows remote consultation and exercise supervision via video call. BodyBalance offers virtual appointments for follow-ups and exercise progression.
Home exercise programs are essential for recovery. BodyBalance provides written instructions, videos, and photos to ensure you can perform exercises correctly at home.
Recovery time varies by condition. Acute injuries may improve in weeks while chronic conditions may take months. BodyBalance sets realistic expectations and monitors progress.
You should see a physiotherapist if you have persistent pain, limited movement, balance problems, or are recovering from surgery or injury. Early intervention often leads to better outcomes.
Physiotherapy is different from massage or chiropractic care. It uses a combination of manual therapy, exercise prescription, and education for comprehensive rehabilitation.
You do not need a doctor's referral to see a physiotherapist at BodyBalance, though some insurance companies may require one for coverage. Contact us to check your specific situation.
How often you should attend physiotherapy depends on your condition. Acute issues may need 2-3 sessions per week initially, while maintenance may be monthly. BodyBalance will advise accordingly.
What you get out of physiotherapy depends on your commitment. Doing home exercises and following advice between sessions significantly improves outcomes. BodyBalance partners with you for success.
The cost of physiotherapy at BodyBalance ranges from 10,000 to 30,000 Naira per session depending on treatment type. Package discounts are available for multiple sessions.
BodyBalance Physiotherapy Clinic is led by Cherry Nwanna, a registered physiotherapist with a Bachelor of Medical Rehabilitation degree and years of experience treating diverse conditions.
The team at BodyBalance includes specialists in sports physiotherapy, neurological rehabilitation, pediatric care, and women's health, ensuring comprehensive expertise for all patients.
BodyBalance Clinic has been serving the Lagos community for several years, building a reputation for quality care and positive patient outcomes.
Parking is available at BodyBalance Clinic for patients who drive. The clinic is also accessible by public transportation.
BodyBalance Clinic is wheelchair accessible with ramps and accessible restrooms. We welcome patients with mobility challenges.
What to bring to your first appointment: identification, referral letter if you have one, medical reports or scans related to your condition, and comfortable clothing for examination.
Can physiotherapy help arthritis? Yes, physiotherapy can help manage arthritis symptoms by improving joint mobility, strengthening supporting muscles, and teaching joint protection strategies.
Is physiotherapy painful? Some treatment techniques may cause temporary discomfort, but physiotherapy should not be painful. Always communicate with your therapist about your comfort level.
How long is a physiotherapy session? Initial assessments at BodyBalance take 45-60 minutes. Follow-up sessions are typically 30-45 minutes depending on treatment needs.
What should I wear to physiotherapy? Wear loose, comfortable clothing that allows easy movement. For lower limb issues, bring shorts. For back issues, a loose top is helpful.
Can I continue sports during physiotherapy? This depends on your injury. Some conditions allow modified activity while others require rest. BodyBalance will advise on safe activity levels.
Does physiotherapy work? Research shows physiotherapy is effective for many musculoskeletal conditions, reducing pain and improving function when delivered by qualified professionals.

What is the difference between physiotherapy and physical therapy? They are the same profession. 'Physiotherapy' is the term used in Nigeria, UK, and Commonwealth countries while 'physical therapy' is used in the USA.

Can children have physiotherapy? Yes, BodyBalance offers pediatric physiotherapy for children with developmental delays, injuries, or neurological conditions. Treatment is age-appropriate and engaging.
Is physiotherapy safe during pregnancy? Yes, BodyBalance provides safe treatment for pregnancy-related pain and discomfort. We use positions and techniques appropriate for each trimester.
Can physiotherapy help headaches? Yes, many headaches are related to neck tension and posture. Physiotherapy addressing these underlying issues can reduce headache frequency and intensity.
What is dry needling? Dry needling is a technique using thin needles to release trigger points in muscles. It is different from acupuncture and is used by physiotherapists for musculoskeletal pain.
What is manual therapy? Manual therapy involves hands-on techniques including joint mobilization, manipulation, and soft tissue massage to reduce pain and improve movement.

What are therapeutic exercises? Therapeutic exercises are specific movements prescribed to address impairments, restore function, and prevent future problems. They are tailored to each patient's needs.
What is a home exercise program? A home exercise program is a set of exercises prescribed for you to do independently between physiotherapy sessions. Compliance with HEP accelerates recovery.
How do I know if physiotherapy is working? Signs of progress include reduced pain, improved range of motion, increased strength, better function in daily activities, and confidence in movement.
Can physiotherapy prevent surgery? In some cases, yes. Good physiotherapy outcomes may eliminate the need for surgery or delay it. However, some conditions do require surgical intervention.
What happens after I finish physiotherapy? BodyBalance provides discharge recommendations including maintenance exercises and advice on preventing recurrence. You can return if problems recur.
BodyBalance offers gift vouchers for physiotherapy services. These make thoughtful gifts for friends or family dealing with pain or recovering from injury.
Corporate wellness programs at BodyBalance include on-site ergonomic assessments, lunch-and-learn sessions on back care, and discounted rates for company employees.
BodyBalance supports local sports teams and running clubs with injury prevention workshops, injury screening, and discounted treatment for members.
